static_findings.description:
Version constraint ^0.8.0 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess
	- AbiReencodingHeadOverflowWithStaticArrayCleanup
	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage
	- DataLocationChangeInInternalOverride
	- NestedCalldataArrayAbiReencodingSizeValidation
	- SignedImmutables
	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory
	- KeccakCaching.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_u8jnwz7k.sol#7)

Low level call in OZManagedVault.withdraw(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_u8jnwz7k.sol#64-70):
	- (ok,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_u8jnwz7k.sol#68)
Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`
